{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Miyazaki-jingū
宮崎神宮 | image = Miyazaki jingu1.jpg | caption = | width = | type = | dedication = [[Emperor Jimmu|Jinmu]]
[[Ugayafukiaezu]]
[[Damayoribime]] | founded = | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = 2-4-1, Jingū, [[Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]]
[[Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki]] 880-0053 | phone = | website = }} {{nihongo|'''Miyazaki-jingū'''|宮崎神宮|}}, located in the city of [[Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]], is a shrine sacred to Japan's first emperor, [[Emperor Jimmu|Jinmu]]. ==Events== Many events are held in the shrine throughout the year. The most imporant event is the '''Aki-no-Taisai''' held in October. This takes the form of a costume parade in honour of Emperor Jinmu, Japan's first emperor, featuring women dressed in gorgeous wedding kimono. In addition, there are several big events per year, including: *On April 3, annual [[Yabusame]] festival is held at this shrine. {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[fr:Miyazaki-jingū]] [[ja:宮崎神宮]]

宮崎神宮 | image = Miyazaki jingu1.jpg | caption = | width = | type = | dedication = [[Emperor Jimmu]]
[[Ugayafukiaezu]]
[[Tamayori-bime]] | founded = | closed = | founder = | priest = | address = 2-4-1, Jingū, [[Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]]
[[Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki]] 880-0053 | phone = | website = http://miyazakijingu.jp/ }} {{nihongo|'''Miyazaki-jingū'''|宮崎神宮|}} is a Shinto shrine located in [[Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]], [[Miyazaki prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to [[Emperor Jimmu]], [[Ugayafukiaezu]] and [[Tamayori-bime]]. ==Events== Many events are held in the shrine throughout the year. The most important event is the '''Aki-no-Taisai''' held in October. This takes the form of a costume parade in honour of Emperor Jinmu, Japan's first emperor, featuring women dressed in gorgeous wedding kimono. In addition, there are several big events per year, including: *On April 3, annual [[Yabusame]] festival {{Commons category|Miyazaki-jingu}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|31|56|19|N|131|25|24|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[fr:Miyazaki-jingū]] [[ja:宮崎神宮]] [[zh:宮崎神宮]]

[[Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki]] 880-0053 | website = {{URL|http://miyazakijingu.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Miyazaki-jingū'''|宮崎神宮|}} is a Shinto shrine located in [[Miyazaki (city)|Miyazaki]], [[Miyazaki Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to [[Emperor Jimmu]], [[Ugayafukiaezu]] and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]]. ==Events== Many events are held at the shrine including the annual [[Yabusame]] festival on April 3. The most important is the Aki-no-Taisai held in October. It is a costume parade in honour of Emperor Jinmu (Japan's first emperor) featuring women dressed in gorgeous wedding kimono. {{Commons category|Miyazaki-jingu}} {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Miyazaki-jingu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei-taisha]]

It is dedicated to [[Emperor Jimmu]], [[Ugayafukiaezu]] and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]]. ==Events== Many events are held at the shrine including the annual [[Yabusame]] festival on April 3. {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Miyazaki, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Miyazaki-jingū
宮崎神宮 | image = Miyazaki jingu1.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|31|56|19|N|131|25|24|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Emperor Jimmu]]
[[Ugayafukiaezu]]
[[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 2-4-1, Jingū, [[Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]]
[[Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki]] 880-0053 | website = {{URL|http://miyazakijingu.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Miyazaki-jingū'''|宮崎神宮|}} is a Shinto shrine located in [[Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]], [[Miyazaki prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to [[Emperor Jimmu]], [[Ugayafukiaezu]] and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]]. ==Events== Many events are held at the shrine including the annual [[Yabusame]] festival on April 3. The most important is the Aki-no-Taisai held in October. It is a costume parade in honour of Emperor Jinmu (Japan's first emperor) featuring women dressed in gorgeous wedding kimono. {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia 2]][[Category:Pages {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Miyazaki, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Miyazaki-jingū
宮崎神宮 | image = Miyazaki jingu1.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|31|56|19|N|131|25|24|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Emperor Jimmu]]
[[Ugayafukiaezu]]
[[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 2-4-1, Jingū, [[Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]]
[[Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki]] 880-0053 | website = {{URL|http://miyazakijingu.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Miyazaki-jingū'''|宮崎神宮|}} is a Shinto shrine located in [[Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]], [[Miyazaki prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to [[Emperor Jimmu]], [[Ugayafukiaezu]] and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]]. ==Events== Many events are held at the shrine including the annual [[Yabusame]] festival on April 3. The most important is the Aki-no-Taisai held in October. It is a costume parade in honour of Emperor Jinmu (Japan's first emperor) featuring women dressed in gorgeous wedding kimono. {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Miyazaki, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Miyazaki-jingū
宮崎神宮 | image = Miyazaki jingu1.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|31|56|19|N|131|25|24|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Emperor Jimmu]]
[[Ugayafukiaezu]]
[[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 2-4-1, Jingū, [[Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]]

It is dedicated to [[Emperor Jimmu]], [[Ugayafukiaezu]] and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]]. ==Events== Many events are held at the shrine including the annual [[Yabusame]] festival on April 3. The most important is the Aki-no-Taisai held in October. It is a costume parade in honour of Emperor Jinmu (Japan's first emperor) featuring women dressed in gorgeous wedding kimono. {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]][[Category:qq {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Miyazaki, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Miyazaki-jingū
宮崎神宮 | image = Miyazaki jingu1.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|31|56|19|N|131|25|24|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Emperor Jimmu]]
[[Ugayafukiaezu]]
[[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 2-4-1, Jingū, [[Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]]
[[Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki]] 880-0053 | website = {{URL|http://miyazakijingu.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Miyazaki-jingū'''|宮崎神宮|}} is a Shinto shrine located in [[Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]], [[Miyazaki prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to [[Emperor Jimmu]], [[Ugayafukiaezu]] and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]]. ==Events== Many events are held at the shrine including the annual [[Yabusame]] festival on April 3. The most important is the Aki-no-Taisai held in October. It is a costume parade in honour of Emperor Jinmu (Japan's first emperor) featuring women dressed in gorgeous wedding kimono. {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Miyazaki, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Miyazaki-jingū
宮崎神宮 | image = Miyazaki jingu1.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|31|56|19|N|131|25|24|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Emperor Jimmu]]
[[Ugayafukiaezu]]
[[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 2-4-1, Jingū, [[Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]]
[[Miyazaki Prefecture|Miyazaki]] 880-0053 | website = {{URL|http://miyazakijingu.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Miyazaki-jingū'''|宮崎神宮|}} is a Shinto shrine located in [[Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]], [[Miyazaki prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is dedicated to [[Emperor Jimmu]], [[Ugayafukiaezu]] and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]]. ==Events== Many events are held at the shrine including the annual [[Yabusame]] festival on April 3. The most important is the Aki-no-Taisai held in October. It is a costume parade in honour of Emperor Jinmu (Japan's first emperor) featuring women dressed in gorgeous wedding kimono. {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia The most important is the Aki-no-Taisai held in October. It is a costume parade in honour of Emperor Jinmu (Japan's first emperor) featuring women dressed in gorgeous wedding kimono. {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Miyazaki-jingu}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] }} [[en:Miyazaki Jingū]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Miyazaki-jingu}} [[eu:Miyazakiko santutegia]] [[fr:Miyazaki-jingū]] [[ja:宮崎神宮]] [[ko:미야자키 신궁]] [[zh:宮崎神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q704686}}

宮崎神宮 | image = Miyazaki jingu1.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|31|56|19|N|131|25|24|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}
[[Ugayafukiaezu]]
[[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]] | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 2-4-1, Jingū, [[:en:Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]]
{{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki}} 880-0053 | website = {{URL|http://miyazakijingu.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Miyazaki-jingū'''|宮崎神宮|}} is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]], {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It is dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}, [[Ugayafukiaezu]] and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]]. ==Events== Many events are held at the shrine including the annual [[Yabusame]] festival on April 3. The most important is the Aki-no-Taisai held in October. It is a costume parade in honour of Emperor Jinmu (Japan's first emperor) featuring women dressed in gorgeous wedding kimono. {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Miyazaki-jingu}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Miyazaki Jingū]] {{Commons category|Miyazaki-jingu}} [[eu:Miyazakiko santutegia]] [[fr:Miyazaki-jingū]] [[ja:宮崎神宮]] [[ko:미야자키 신궁]] [[zh:宮崎神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q704686}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} 880-0053 | website = {{URL|http://miyazakijingu.jp/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Miyazaki-jingū'''|宮崎神宮|}} is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Miyazaki, Miyazaki|Miyazaki]], {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|qid=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It is dedicated to {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, [[Ugayafukiaezu]] and [[Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Tamayori-bime]]. ==Events== Many events are held at the shrine including the annual [[Yabusame]] festival on April 3. The most important is the Aki-no-Taisai held in October. It is a costume parade in honour of Emperor Jinmu (Japan's first emperor) featuring women dressed in gorgeous wedding kimono. {{Commons Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 2]] [[Category|Miyazaki-jingu}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Commons category|Miyazaki-jingu}} {{wikidata link|Q704686|co|commons:Category:Miyazaki-jingu|en|Miyazaki Jingū|eu|Miyazakiko santutegia|fr|Miyazaki-jingū|ja|宮崎神宮|ko|미야자키 신궁|zh|宮崎神宮}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
